title: Isolate nodes so a failure degrades safely
description: Structure the ROS graph so one node crashing or stalling triggers a safe response instead of silent loss of control.
body: Use when designing the ROS/ROS2 node and topic graph. Do not put unrelated critical functions in one node, and avoid assuming a publisher is alive just because a subscriber exists. Actions: split responsibilities into focused nodes, set QoS (reliability/deadline) appropriate to each topic, add liveliness/heartbeat monitoring, and define what the system does when a critical node goes silent (stop, hold, or safe degrade). Validation signals include a test where killing a critical node triggers the defined safe response within its deadline. Risk boundary: a stalled node that silently stops publishing commands can leave actuators in their last state and cause harm; treat missing liveness handling on a control-critical topic as a safety defect.
